channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: BLG Toplaner Bin Fined $22K for Failing to High-Five Fans
item.content:encoded:
Bilibili Gaming toplaner Chen ‘Bin’ Ze-Bin has been handed a heavy fine by Riot Games for not interacting with LPL fans.

BLG are competing in the chaotic LPL Split 2 Playoffs in the Lower Bracket after a surprise loss to major underdogs Team WE.

After BLG defeated EDward Gaming 3-0 on Wednesday, Bin did not go over to high-five fans who had travelled to watch the series.

Fans are believed to have paid for VIP tickets, which advertised player interactions as part of the package.

As a result, Riot fined Bin ¥150,000 RMB (~22k) and also handed his team BLG an additional ¥50,000 RMB (~7.4k) fine.

Bin will also have to participate in relevant education and training following the incident.

LoL Esports Bad Player Behaviour Epidemic

The League of Legends publisher has handed out a lot of fines and punishments this year.

The official statement from Riot and the LPL on the Bin incident read:

“Bin failed to fulfil his on-stage interaction obligations as required by official match-day protocols.

“This conduct violates the professional standards expected of an LPL player, disrupts the standardized operational order of the competition, and has negatively impacted the viewing experience and enthusiasm of the audience.”

A similar incident happened earlier this year in Korea with superstar ADC Nam ‘Diable’ Dae-geun.

BNK FEARX dropped Diable from its LCK line-up for skipping a fan meet, he then proceeded to leave the team and join Nongshim RedDorce.

As for the Bin incident, Riot found BLG negligent in its pre-match player management and behavioral guidance and was therefore held partially responsible for the pro’s behaviour – hence their fine.

Riot also fined LEC organisations Movistar KOI and Karmine Corp, as well as Canadian midlaner Joseph Joon ‘Jojopyun’ Pyun this week.

Jojopyun was fined €5,000 for swearing during his on-air interview during the LEC Roadtrip in Madrid.

Meanwhile, MKOI and KCorp were fined €2k each for the heated online argument between the two teams’ owners.

Does Riot need to do more to have esports pros treat fans better? Let us know your opinions on the Insider Gaming Discord.

On the flip side, LoL fans can support their favourite players when the T1 Worlds winners 2025 skins and new Hall of Legends skins release.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Esports Nations Cup Revaels 107 MLBB Rosters
item.content:encoded:
The Esports Foundation has revealed 107 officially submitted rosters for Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB) at the Esports Nations Cup.

MLBB was by far the most-watched event at the Esports World Cup 2025, with over 3 million peak viewers, with PUBG Mobile next at just 1.38m.

While 107 countries have submitted teams, only 32 will qualify for the Esports Nations Cup, with 16 earning direct invites to the $1.5m event.

Players’ recent performances will earn their team points to decide the invitees, but MLBB invites won’t be sent out until August 2.

Another 14 teams will qualify through regional online qualifiers, with the final two slots awarded via ENC Wildcards.

The Top MLBB ENC Teams

While invites won’t be sent out for another couple of months, the current MLBB ENC rankings and scores are as follows:

  • Philippines – 10,400.0 – KarlTzy / Jaypee / Sanji / FlapTzy / Super Marco / Kelra / Kairi
  • Indonesia – 6,150.0 – Yazukee / Arfy / alekk / Lutpiii / S A N Z
  • Myanmar – 5,200.0 – Blink / Hannn / Ying / Niko / Alitaaa / Bo Ye / Rixum Leo
  • Cambodia – 4,100.0 – SAA / Felix / PP / Linnn / Wickyy
  • Türkiye – 3,950.0 – Tienzy / Sigibum / Rosa / EKSI / Blotzfet / Rx / Intangible
  • Egypt – 3,700.0 – KENJI / Speedy / Quanok / Seven Up / Shen / Smile / Throwboy
  • Saudi Arabia – 3,400.0 – Arshven / K7 / Saano / Trolll / Cuffin / Tiger / Heroshi
  • Mongolia – 3,140.0 – Zxaura / Mayki / Kei / Oyu / Bankai / Stillmeta / Bright
  • People’s Republic of China – 3,100.0 – Loong / Swim / Unicorn / Modi / Korhae / Zaoshui / FishGod
  • India – 2,600.0 – Abhi / Kyle / ROMCOM / Vann / APEX / Kimyy / J
  • Singapore – 2,010.0 – Lolsie / Vanix / yaawn / Diablo / Roy / BRAYYY / Gear
  • Germany – 1,750.0 – Kid Bomba / Saiki / Hangiii / boro / Haruto / Mahoraga / Silas
  • United States of America – 1,750.0 – bestplayer1 / Yato / Melon / Cole World / Mercyless / G The Aiko
  • Brazil – 1,725.0 – Kiing / Frostt / Gustalagusta / Akashi / Vaguin / Upa / Tekashi
  • Canada – 1,675.0 – ZIA / Shiro / RR / Legendo / Song / RYLAI
  • Argentina– 1,675.0 – Pakitoo / Arctic / Furyy / Jotun / Feshin / Deez / Alzz

The Philippines are miles ahead of the competition, featuring a roster made up of the core from the reigning EWC champions Team Liquid Philippines, joined by players from Team Falcons Philippines.

Meanwhile, Falcons’ MENA trio of Saano, Trolll, and Cuffin will lead the 7th-ranked Saudi Arabia squad.

Can anyone stop the Philippines roster? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Meanwhile, the Esports Foundation has also confirmed 88 Dota 2 Esports Nations Cup rosters.

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Thresh and Senna Revealed for 2XKO With June 9 Release
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has revealed two more playable fighters for 2XKO in Senna and Thresh, who will release at the same time.

Senna and Thresh will release in 2XKO on June 9, taking the fighting game up to 15 characters.

Earlier this year, Riot claimed five new fighters would be added to the game in 2026 and have already added Caitlyn and Akali this year.

Senna and Thresh share a bitter past from their League of Legends lore, yet they can team up together in 2XKO.

It has also been confirmed by Riot that both champions will be usable for competitors at 2XKO EVO 2026, part of the biggest FGC event of the year.

Thresh in 2XKO

Thresh brings his unique LoL kit to the fighting game, constantly hooking in enemies from afar and can spawn his teammates to his lantern’s location.

His official description reads:

“Wicked and cunning, Thresh is a malevolent specter that delights in tormenting opponents, imprisoning their souls in his lantern for all eternity.

“He uses his chain to attack from tricky and unexpected angles, while his lantern lets him teleport and summon jailed souls for aid.”

He also brings a brand new archetype to the game in Strategist, but Riot Games has not shared any further details on what this means.

Perhaps it pertains to planning ahead with lantern placement, or simply is a character that relies on teamwork.

Senna in 2XKO

Meanwhile, Senna has two distinct playstyles in 2XKO, used her Relic Cannon to shoot at opponents from range or turning into her wraith mode, born from years of soul being held captive by Thresh, to put the weapon away and fight in a melee form.

Senna’s official description says:

“A champion of the light cursed by darkness, Senna blasts foes with her vaunted relic cannon before rushing them down as a shadowy wraith. With two forms to choose from, she’s highly capable of redeeming any corrupted soul.

Senna also brings a new archetype to the game in hybrid, seeing as she works as both a zoner and a rushdown character.

What other LoL champions would you like to see added to 2XKO? Share your ideas with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Additionally, the fighting game will also have its first team-based esports tournament later this year in 2XKO Red Bull Knockout.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Amazon Says It’s ‘Too Early To Discuss’ Future 007 Titles
item.content:encoded:
Recently, Amazon provided an update regarding the future of James Bond video games, as it was suggested that new titles will be solely published by Amazon, not IO Interactive. This sentiment arrived following the standout success of 007 First Light, with the game selling over 1.5 million copies in just 24 hours; you’d imagine that this is just the beginning for the series.

Well, in the latest statement by Amazon Game Studios, it’s been deemed ‘too early to discuss future projects.’ Instead, IO will have more to share regarding 007 First Light in the near future: any further than that seems to be unknown.

What Does 007’s Future Look Like Under Amazon & MGM?

The new statement from Amazon was received by VGC, and it was in response to the notion that future 007 titles would be ‘done by MGM and, theoretically, by Amazon Game Studios.’

That sentiment created a concern that IO Interactive would lose out on the ability to create future 007 titles, as it seemed like the resounding notion was that Amazon were in control, and IO would instead take the back seat. This hit especially hard after the resounding success and impact First Light has had.

In response to this concern, Amazon responded with this:

Amazon MGM holds the rights to any future James Bond video games but it’s still too early to discuss future projects.

We have a great relationship with IO Interactive and are proud of what we’ve accomplished together on 007 First Light. Our partners at IO will reveal more about 007 First Light in the near future and we’re looking forward to sharing what’s next.

This new statement doesn’t paint much of a clearer picture, but it was made abundantly clear that, whilst it’s too early to discuss future projects in the Bond franchise, Amazon has a ‘great relationship with IO Interactive.’ It doesn’t seem like they’re desperate to cut IO off, and the likelihood is that Amazon will simply take over on the publishing of the game, with IO in charge of development.

Still, as Amazon said itself, it’s too early to paint a specific picture, but with the commercial and critical success of First Light, you’d imagine some important conversations are taking place regarding Bond’s future—after all, a sequel was supposedly dependent on public reception to First Light, and could that be any better amid GOTY shouts?

What do you hope to see for Bond’s future? Should Amazon just let IO get on with it themselves? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Subnautica 2’s first major update, as well as the 50% drop in PS5 sales in the UK.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
